read.voice_policy: Direct argumentative prose derived from the lecture. Avoid repeated third-person framing. Do not impersonate first-person authorship except when quoting verified source material.
read.trace_policy: Paragraph-level transcript refs. Speculative claims stay marked as speculative.
read.dek: The apparent U.S.-Iran war is recast as an imperial succession crisis. America tries to negotiate with bombs, Iran turns the war into economic and narrative leverage, and Israel auditions to become the new muscle of the system.
read.thesis: The lecture's central move is a reversal: this is not simply America fighting Iran. It is a system preparing to replace a failing American empire. Once America cannot win through decapitation, cannot reflect, cannot manufacture enough, and cannot accept casualties, the global order needs another empire to provide muscle. Jiang names that replacement Pax Judaica: Israel as the new Middle Eastern power, paired not with Iran's destruction but with an Iran strong enough to split the region, tax Hormuz, and help force America home.

opening.heading: Core Reading
opening.text: The war begins, in Jiang's account, with American hubris. Washington expects decapitation, surrender, and a clean narrative of victory. Instead Iran fights back, Gulf states are hit, oil becomes the hinge of the world economy, and cheap drones expose American bases as fragile. The Pentagon's line is still: we are winning, we do not need to negotiate, we negotiate with bombs. The harder claim is that this blindness is exactly why America loses. Iran is not trying to make the world conform to one military plan. Iran uses military moves to reshape economics, politics, and narrative. That reversal opens the larger question: if America is no longer reliable imperial muscle, who replaces it?

beats.heading: Four-Dimensional War
beats.summary: America makes narrative, politics, and economics obey a military plan; Iran makes military action serve narrative, politics, and economics.

paragraphs.text: War has four dimensions: narrative, political, economic, and military. America's mistake is to make the first three obey the fourth. The military plan is decapitation and forced surrender, so NATO is supposed to open Hormuz, the economy is supposed to absorb low oil prices, and the media is supposed to report victory. Everything must bend toward the military story.

beats.heading: Two Regional Powers
beats.summary: The predicted order does not require Iran's destruction: Israel and Iran become strong poles while the GCC splits and weak players look for patrons.

paragraphs.text: The new order leaves two powers: Israel and Iran. Israel does not need to erase Iran because Persia is outside the Greater Israel project. Israel needs Iran weakened enough and America removed enough. It wants the resources, routes, and command position of the Middle East; the Americans are in the way.
